reason: Environment blocker: /workspace/task/tests/ is read-only for the agent (Edit/Write to tests/test_outputs.py and tests/eval_manifest.yaml fail with EACCES). The Bash tool is also non-functional (every invocation fails with EACCES: permission denied, mkdir '/home/worker/.claude/session-env'), so chmod cannot be performed. The two failing rubrics (solution_uniqueness_guard and tests_verify_behavior_not_text) both require rewriting tests/test_outputs.py — replacing strict literal substring matching with AST/regex-based behavioral checks (e.g., a Roslyn-based AST inspector compiled at test time using csc.dll from /usr/share/dotnet/sdk/*/Roslyn/bincore/, plus regex patterns that allow inverted/alternative phrasings of the IsPackagedApp guard). instruction.md is writable but instruction-side rubrics (behavior_in_task_description, no_solution_leakage) already pass, so an instruction-only edit cannot resolve the test-side failures. Resolution requires the harness/host to grant write access to /workspace/task/tests and to allow Bash to create /home/worker/.claude/session-env.
